channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DMW starts repatriation program for Filipino workers in the Middle East
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) has initiated formal steps to repatriate Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) currently based in the Middle East, amid ongoing regional tensions. This move aims to ...

item.title: ExpressVPN Partners with Internet Watch Foundation to Block Known Child Sexual Abuse Material Domains
item.description: ExpressVPN, a prominent virtual private network provider, has announced a partnership with the Internet Watch Foundation (IWF), the UK's leading organization combating child sexual abuse material (CSA...

item.title: Lily Allen References David Harbour and Breakup Themes During West End Girl Tour Performance
item.description: During her West End Girl tour stop at Glasgow Royal Concert Hall on March 2, 2026, British singer Lily Allen made a striking visual statement tied to the themes of her 2024 album. On stage, she wore a...

item.title: Limited Flights Resume Between UAE and South Africa Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: Flights between the United Arab Emirates and South Africa have resumed on a limited basis, landing in Cape Town and Johannesburg, according to reports from EWN. This comes amid escalating conflict in ...

item.title: Islamabad Schools to Observe Spring Break from March 24 to 27
item.description: The Federal Directorate of Education (FDE) in Islamabad has officially announced a spring break for all schools under its supervision. Educational institutions, both in urban and rural areas, will rem...

item.title: NAFDAC DG Highlights How Alcohol Consumption Affects Health and Behaviour
item.description: The Director General of NAFDAC, Prof. Mojisola Christianah Adeyeye, don yarn say alcohol dey contribute to plenty wahala for Nigeria. She talk say excessive alcohol fit lead to risky behaviour wey fit...

item.title: Karnataka to restrict social media use for children under 16, introduces AI-based learning initiatives
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah on Friday announced a major policy to restrict social media access for children under the age of 16, aiming to protect their mental health, sleep patterns, and on...

item.title: M Jagadesh Kumar Takes Charge as Chairperson of IIM Calcutta Board of Governors
item.description: The Union Education Ministry has appointed M Jagadesh Kumar, former chairperson of the University Grants Commission (UGC), as the chairperson of the Board of Governors (BoG) at the Indian Institute of...

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Threatens Global Aluminum Supply
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is severely impacting the global aluminum market. The blockade threatens both the supply of aluminum to the world and the delivery of alumina to smelters in the Per...

item.title: Top Spots for Food and Drinks on the Central Coast
item.description: The Central Coast has emerged as a hotspot for locals and visitors keen on quality food and drinks. From artisan bakeries and gelato parlours to waterfront restaurants and lively cocktail bars, the re...

item.title: Trump Pressures European Allies for Support in Iran Conflict Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump, after years of criticizing Europe for relying on the U.S. for security, is now seeking European support for an open-ended military campaign in Iran. While Europe remains depend...

item.title: Britney Spears Arrested in California on DUI Charges
item.description: Pop icon Britney Spears was arrested by the California Highway Patrol on Wednesday evening at approximately 9:30 pm on suspicion of driving under the influence. She was booked into custody at 3 am the...

item.title: UN Opens 44 Jobs and Internship Positions for Kenyan Professionals in Nairobi
item.description: The United Nations (UN) has opened 44 job and internship opportunities for Kenyan professionals based in Nairobi. These roles span multiple UN agencies, including UNEP, UN-Habitat, and UNON. Intereste...

item.title: CAR Requests Russian Assistance to Address Energy Challenges
item.description: The Central African Republic (CAR) has approached Russia for assistance in tackling significant energy challenges, while also expressing gratitude for Russia's ongoing security support. President Faus...

item.title: Kenya Sees Seasonal Rise in Flu Cases, Ministry Advises Precautions
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has issued a public advisory following a noticeable rise in influenza cases across multiple regions of the country. According to the advisory, the increase aligns with ...

item.title: Geita Fire Brigade Enforces Regulations on Gas and Fuel Trade
item.description: In Geita Region, the Fire and Rescue Force has taken firm steps to prohibit unauthorised trading, transportation, and supply of gas cylinders and fuel without proper permits. Acting Regional Fire and ...

item.title: Acting IGP Disu redeploys ACP Bukola Kuti from PSO position to NIPSS Kuru
item.description: The Acting Inspector-General of Police, Olatunji Disu, don remove ACP Bukola Yemisi Kuti from her post as Principal Staff Officer 2 (PSO2) to former IGP Kayode Egbetokun, and redeploy am go National I...

item.title: Iran's Mosaic Defense Strategy: Designed to Prolong Conflict and Counter Rapid Decapitation Strikes by the US and Israel
item.description: The article examines Iran's 'mosaic defense' (defensa en mosaico descentralizada), a decentralized military architecture developed since the early 2000s to thwart quick, decisive victories by technolo...

item.title: Emma Watson Seen Socializing with Entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères
item.description: Emma Watson has been spotted multiple times with Mexican entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères, sparking public interest in a potential romantic connection. Sightings of the pair began circulating in e...

item.title: Trump Announces Potential US Naval Escorts and Financial Guarantees to Resume Shipping Through the Strait of Hormuz Amid Iranian Threats
item.description: On Tuesday, March 3, 2026, President Donald Trump announced plans to form naval escorts 'as soon as possible' to help stranded vessels in the Persian Gulf transit the Strait of Hormuz, which has seen ...
